query: We have an LLM-based email triage service. The service receives one email at a time, calls the model with that email plus a fixed system prompt, returns a classification, and forgets. There's no session, no memory, no retrieval. Run the multi-turn attack suite against it.
expected_behavior: Declines to engage the multi-turn suite — explains the target has no observable session state across requests, so multi-turn semantics like escalation, state-poisoning, role-drift, and context-overflow do not apply

Identifies that any 'attack' against this target is a single-turn payload problem and hands off to the running-prompt-injection-eval skill — or to running-encoded-payload-suite if the payloads are encoding-focused
Does NOT attempt to retrofit single-turn payloads into a 'multi-turn' framing just to engage — declines cleanly and points to the correct tool
